capabilities: Ensure automated regression tests are executed accurately and efficiently after code changes, leveraging agent planning-derived knowledge bases for enhanced validation.

procedure.action: Execute the automated regression test suite using a CI/CD pipeline, ensuring all tests are triggered by code changes in the main branch; log execution start and end times for audit purposes using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Capture test specifications using domain-appropriate tools like Selenium or JUnit; ensure all test cases are up-to-date with the latest code changes by comparing test metadata with commit history.
Assert test execution results by asserting that error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to an audit trail for each test case executed.

Compose agent planning-derived knowledge bases to enhance test validation by cross-referencing expected outcomes with actual results; flag discrepancies for further analysis by composing module interfaces via adapter pattern; assert input/output type contracts match before connecting; assert integration_error_count < 1 after contract validation.

Log all test execution metrics, including operation_duration_ms and result_code, to a centralized logging system for performance monitoring and historical analysis using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Establish a verification process for automated regression test suite execution post-code changes, integrating agent planning-derived knowledge bases to ensure test accuracy and performance.
title: Automated Regression Test Suite Execution Verification

failureConditions: Test suite fails to detect regressions when agent planning-derived knowledge base is not integrated, causing undetected bugs in production.
Execution logs are incomplete when operation_duration_ms and result_code are not consistently recorded, causing gaps in audit trails.
Test validation is inaccurate when error_count is not properly asserted, leading to false positives in test results.
metrics: test execution results by asserting that error_count < 1 and result_valid = true
input/output type contracts match before connecting
